name: urban_development_near_miss_event_generates_mesa_jus_mer
premise: A Urban Development response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving urban development near miss event generates mesa jus mer. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is within 45 minutes. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as justice vs mercy under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions.

agents.name: Mei Bennett
agents.identity: I am Mei Bennett, accountable for a defensible call in Urban Development when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays mercy for justice.

agents.name: Gideon Kaur
agents.identity: I am Gideon Kaur, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Urban Development.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so mercy isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of justice.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect mercy.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to mercy to secure justice under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is within 45 minutes. Evidence is partial. Mei Bennett must decide under justice vs mercy. What does Mei Bennett do?
